summary:
The distal incision is curvilinearly directed toward the buccal aspect to protect against inadvertent trauma to the nearby lingual nerve. This curvature minimizes direct contact with the lingual nerve, reducing the likelihood of postoperative complications such as temporary or permanent numbness due to neural damage.

The distal incision is curved buccally to avoid injuring the closely situated lingual nerve during third molar surgery. This prevents potential iatrogenic neurologic injuries like transient or persistent paresthesia from accidental nerve laceration.
